description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting sharply with the lighter, speckled stone material, which appears to be granite or a similar hard rock. The block's sides are smooth and solid black, giving it a modern, industrial look. The top surface's grooved pattern suggests it may serve as a drainage or anti-slip feature, commonly found in urban environments. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to act as a barrier or obstacle in public spaces. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on the stone.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its speckled granite top and smooth black sides, likely used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved, speckled stone top with the grooves appearing as horizontal dark lines.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved stone top with horizontal dark lines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the contrast between the stone and the dark gaps.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or anti-slip platform
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
